# JSValueToObject(_:_:_:)

**Function**

Converts a JavaScript value to an object and returns the resulting object.

## Declaration

```swift
func JSValueToObject(_ ctx: JSContextRef!, _ value: JSValueRef!, _ exception: UnsafeMutablePointer<JSValueRef?>!) -> JSObjectRef!
```

## Parameters

- **ctx**: The execution context to use.
- **value**: The [doc://com.apple.javascriptcore/documentation/JavaScriptCore/JSValueRef] to convert.
- **exception**: A pointer to a [doc://com.apple.javascriptcore/documentation/JavaScriptCore/JSValueRef] to store an exception in, if any. Pass `NULL` to discard any exception.

## Return Value

The [doc://com.apple.javascriptcore/documentation/JavaScriptCore/JSObjectRef] result of conversion, or `NULL` if the system throws an exception.

## Converting to Primitive Values

- **JSValueToBoolean(_:_:)**: Converts a JavaScript value to a Boolean and returns the resulting Boolean.
- **JSValueToNumber(_:_:_:)**: Converts a JavaScript value to a number and returns the resulting number.
- **JSValueToStringCopy(_:_:_:)**: Converts a JavaScript value to a string and copies the result into a JavaScript string.
